author: lOrm
content:
Forensic evidence — fix applied in src/tools/file/read.rs.

Layer 1 (pre-spawn guard): new helper reject_flag_prefix(path) refuses any path string whose first character is '-'. Catches -help, --version, -opw, -upw, -f, -l, --nocenter, and every other pdftotext/pdfinfo flag form. Both read_pdf_file branches (no-pages and with-pages) call this guard at function entry, BEFORE any Command::new is built.

Layer 2 (option terminator): both subprocess invocations now pass '--' immediately before the path argument:
  Command::new("pdfinfo").arg("--").arg(path).output()
  cmd.arg("--"); cmd.arg(path); cmd.arg("-");   // pdftotext
This is defence-in-depth — even if layer 1 were ever bypassed, '--' guarantees the path cannot be re-parsed as a flag.

8 new tests pin both layers (Behavior 10 block, lines 762-862 of read.rs):
  1. reject_flag_prefix_rejects_single_dash_filename       (-help)
  2. reject_flag_prefix_rejects_double_dash_filename       (--version)
  3. reject_flag_prefix_rejects_password_flag_filename     (-opw, -upw — the consume-next-argv flags)
  4. reject_flag_prefix_accepts_absolute_path              (/tmp/normal.pdf)
  5. reject_flag_prefix_accepts_dot_slash_relative_path    (./doc.pdf, subdir/doc.pdf)
  6. read_pdf_file_rejects_leading_hyphen_filename         (end-to-end '-help')
  7. read_pdf_file_rejects_password_flag_filename_with_pages (end-to-end '-opw' with pages='1-3')
  8. read_pdf_file_uses_double_dash_terminator_in_source   (source-level invariant for both call sites)

Verification:
  cargo test --lib                     -> 1295 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored  (baseline 1287 + 8 new)
  cargo clippy --lib --tests -- -D warnings  -> exit 0, no warnings
  grep -nE '#\[(allow|ignore)' new code   -> none
  ls clippy.toml                       -> No such file or directory

Out of scope (mandated refactor items deferred — should file follow-up):
  - pdfinfo regex anchoring (line.starts_with("Pages:") still loose; mandated as anchored ^Pages:\s+(\d+)$)
  - max output size on pdftotext stdout (truncation guard still applied only at read_text_file)
  - max page-span enforcement (>200 pages)
These were listed in the mandated refactor but are functional-quality items separate from the flag-injection vector closed here.
